reasoning: I will vote for Biswajit Das and TMC, though my hand trembles as I mark the button. I saw in 2020 that Mamata-didi cannot stop the corruption in our own panchayat, that the wall she built does not reach down to protect us from our neighbors' greed. But I look at Shantanu Thakur and I think of CAA, of NRC, of the asymmetry that leaves Muslim families like mine vulnerable if the documents are ever demanded. The BJP candidate is now a minister in Delhi—he brings that ideology here, into our village. I cannot vote for that, no matter how disappointed I am. My local problems are serious, but they are not existential. My faith in Mamata is cracked, but my fear of the alternative is stronger.
primary_drivers: Fear that BJP-led NRC implementation would target Muslim citizens without NRC protection
Disillusionment with TMC's capacity to prevent local corruption and protect vulnerable families
Concern that incumbent BJP MP represents Hindu-majoritarian communal politics despite minister-level position
